final_answer:
What’s left for BSC parity: election rules, slashing/jail rules, rewards/economics, and governance/epoch behavior are not yet fully parity-validated end-to-end.

Why this matters: until those gates are validated against a pinned BSC baseline, “parity” is not proven and chain behavior can still diverge in production-critical economics/safety paths.

What should be done next:
1. Lock one exact BSC baseline version and parity checklist.
2. Validate each remaining domain gate-by-gate (election, slashing, rewards, governance/epochs).
3. Run differential tests and mark parity complete only when every gate passes.
prompt: WHAT THE FUCK IS NOW LEFT WITH BSC PARITY?
